title: Oldenlandia megalantha
taxonomy: Oldenlandia megalantha is classified within the family Rubiaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee family. This plant is further classified within the genus Oldenlandia, which consists of approximately 150 species of annual or perennial herbs. The species Oldenlandia megalantha itself is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The classification of Oldenlandia megalantha reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family. Further classification may also involve the identification of its specific cultivar or variety.
uses: Oldenlandia megalantha is known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves are also used as a treatment for wounds and to reduce inflammation. In addition, Oldenlandia megalantha is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses.
benefits: Oldenlandia megalantha has been studied for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It may help in reducing oxidative stress and improving overall health. Some research suggests that Oldenlandia megalantha may have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in fighting off infections. Additionally, it may have anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm this. Further studies are required to fully understand the potential health benefits of Oldenlandia megalantha.

It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Studies have shown that the plant's extracts exhibit potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
